segments.text: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel, he went not, as at other times, to seek for enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.

footnotes: to seek…: Heb. to the meeting of

segments.text: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents according to their tribes; and the spirit of God came upon him.
And he took up his parable, and said,
Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: whose…: Heb. who had his eyes shut, but now opened

segments.text: He hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: How goodly are thy tents, O Jacob, and thy tabernacles, O Israel!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: As the valleys are they spread forth, as gardens by the river's side, as the trees of lign aloes which the LORD hath planted, and as cedar trees beside the waters.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: He shall pour the water out of his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters, and his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God brought him forth out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn: he shall eat up the nations his enemies, and shall break their bones, and pierce them through with his arrows.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: He couched, he lay down as a lion, and as a great lion: who shall stir him up? Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balak's anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together:
I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them these three times.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honour; but, lo, the LORD hath kept thee back from honour.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: And Balaam said unto Balak,
Spake I not also to thy messengers which thou sentest unto me, saying,
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the commandment of the LORD, to do either good or bad of mine own mind; but what the LORD saith, that will I speak?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come therefore, and I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And he took up his parable, and said,
Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: He hath said, which heard the words of God, and knew the knowledge of the most High, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him, but not nigh: there shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre shall rise out of Israel, and shall smite the corners of Moab, and destroy all the children of Sheth.
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: smite…: or, smite through the princes of

segments.text: And Edom shall be a possession, Seir also shall be a possession for his enemies; and Israel shall do valiantly.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Out of Jacob shall come he that shall have dominion, and shall destroy him that remaineth of the city.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And when he looked on Amalek, he took up his parable, and said,
Amalek was the first of the nations; but his latter end shall be that he perish for ever.
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: the nations: the nations that warred against Israel
heading: Prophecy Against Amalek

segments.text: And he looked on the Kenites, and took up his parable, and said,
Strong is thy dwellingplace, and thou puttest thy nest in a rock.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Nevertheless the Kenite shall be wasted, until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: until…: or, how long shall it be ere Asshur carry thee away captive

segments.text: And he took up his parable, and said,
Alas, who shall live when God doeth this!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And ships shall come from the coast of Chittim, and shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber, and he also shall perish for ever.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place: and Balak also went his way.
